name: actions
context: Configure and trigger GitHub Actions CI/CD workflows
info: This skill covers GitHub Actions: defining YAML workflows in `.github/workflows/`, configuring triggers (push, pull_request, schedule, workflow_dispatch), managing secrets, using matrix strategies for multi-environment testing, and storing build artifacts. Agents use this skill to set up CI pipelines, trigger workflow runs programmatically via the API, monitor run status, and download artifacts. Sub-skills provide focused coverage: [[github/actions/workflows]] for YAML definition, [[github/actions/runs]] for run management, and [[github/actions/artifacts]] for build output. Actions integrate tightly with [[github/pulls]] for required status checks and [[github/repos/settings]] for merge gating. See [[github/auth]] for authentication.
body:
# GitHub Actions

GitHub Actions is the built-in CI/CD platform. Workflows are defined in YAML files under `.github/workflows/` and run on GitHub-hosted or self-hosted runners. Actions can build, test, deploy, and automate any software workflow.

## Sub-Skills

This skill is divided into focused sub-skills:

- **[[github/actions/workflows]]** — Define workflow YAML with triggers, jobs, matrix strategies, and reusable workflows
- **[[github/actions/runs]]** — List, view, trigger, re-run, and cancel workflow runs
- **[[github/actions/artifacts]]** — Upload and download build artifacts between jobs and externally

## Workflow Syntax

```yaml
# .github/workflows/ci.yml
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm test

  lint:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run lint
```

## Triggering Workflows via API

```javascript
import { Octokit } from "@octokit/rest";

const octokit = new Octokit({ auth: process.env.GITHUB_TOKEN });

// Trigger a workflow_dispatch event
await octokit.actions.createWorkflowDispatch({
  owner: "my-org",
  repo: "my-project",
  workflow_id: "deploy.yml",
  ref: "main",
  inputs: {
    environment: "production",
    version: "1.2.3",
  },
});

// List workflow runs
const { data: runs } = await octokit.actions.listWorkflowRuns({
  owner: "my-org",
  repo: "my-project",
  workflow_id: "ci.yml",
  status: "completed",
  per_page: 5,
});

// Get a specific run
const { data: run } = await octokit.actions.getWorkflowRun({
  owner: "my-org",
  repo: "my-project",
  run_id: 12345,
});
console.log(run.status, run.conclusion, run.html_url);
```

## Secrets Management

Store sensitive values as repository or organization secrets:

```javascript
import sodium from "libsodium-wrappers";

// Encrypt and set a secret
await sodium.ready;
const { data: key } = await octokit.actions.getRepoPublicKey({
  owner: "my-org",
  repo: "my-project",
});

const encrypted = sodium.crypto_box_seal(
  Buffer.from("secret-value"),
  Buffer.from(key.key, "base64")
);

await octokit.actions.createOrUpdateRepoSecret({
  owner: "my-org",
  repo: "my-project",
  secret_name: "API_KEY",
  encrypted_value: Buffer.from(encrypted).toString("base64"),
  key_id: key.key_id,
});
```

Access secrets in workflows via `${{ secrets.API_KEY }}`.

## Matrix Strategy

```yaml
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        node-version: [18, 20, 22]
        os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest]
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ matrix.node-version }}
      - run: npm ci && npm test
```

## Artifacts

```yaml
# Upload build artifacts
- uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
  with:
    name: build-output
    path: dist/

# Download in another job
- uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
  with:
    name: build-output
```

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Purpose |
|----------|----------|
| `GITHUB_TOKEN` | Auto-provided in workflows, or PAT for API calls |

sections.title: Workflow YAML syntax
sections.body: Workflows live in `.github/workflows/*.yml`. Define `name`, `on` (triggers), and `jobs`. Each job specifies `runs-on` (runner), `steps` (actions and commands), and optionally `needs` (job dependencies). Steps use `uses:` for marketplace actions or `run:` for shell commands. See [[github/repos]] for repository setup.

sections.title: Workflow triggers
sections.body: Common triggers: `push` (branch pushes), `pull_request` (PR events, integrates with [[github/pulls]]), `schedule` (cron), `workflow_dispatch` (manual/API trigger), `issues` (reacts to [[github/issues]] events), `release` (release events). Filter branches and paths with `branches:` and `paths:`. Use `workflow_dispatch` with `inputs:` for parameterized runs.

sections.title: Secrets management
sections.body: Store secrets at repo or org level. Access in workflows via `${{ secrets.NAME }}`. Set via API with `octokit.actions.createOrUpdateRepoSecret()` — requires encrypting with the repo's public key (libsodium). Secrets are masked in logs. Environment secrets scope access to specific deployment environments. See [[github/auth]] for token management.

sections.title: Matrix strategy
sections.body: Define `strategy.matrix` to run a job across multiple configurations (Node versions, OSes, environments). GitHub creates a job instance for each combination. Use `fail-fast: false` to continue other matrix jobs when one fails. Use `include:` and `exclude:` for fine-grained control over combinations.

sections.title: Build artifacts
sections.body: Upload artifacts with `actions/upload-artifact@v4` and download with `actions/download-artifact@v4`. Artifacts persist for 90 days by default. Use artifacts to pass build outputs between jobs or preserve test reports. Download via API with `octokit.actions.downloadArtifact()`.

scripts.name: Trigger Workflow
scripts.description: Trigger a GitHub Actions workflow dispatch event via the REST API

GITHUB_TOKEN.description: GitHub personal access token or installation token

workflowId.description: Workflow file name or ID (e.g. deploy.yml)

ref.description: Git ref to run the workflow on (branch, tag, SHA)

runUrl.description: HTML URL of the workflow run
